digest.component_or_area: claude-skills shared test-design standard and skill integration
propositions: Create a canonical shared test-design.md standard covering coverage inventory, contract card, tier selection, trigger/assert rule, oracle minimum, contrast case, fault realism, gating proof, defect-seeded mutation, and LLM evals, with the scale-to-the-bug principle explicit.
Each of the three skill directories ships a real file test-design.md byte-identical to the canonical copy, using same-directory companion-file references and no symlinks.
A gating unit test test_skill_shared_sync.py asserts byte-identity of each copy against the canonical file and fails when a copy diverges or is missing, collected under make test.

rebar-debug SKILL.md Step 4 is retitled to RED-oracle design, its LLM-surface paragraph defers to the shared quantified-eval standard, and Step 5 adds a defect-seeded mutation check plus a contract-card fourth handoff item without exposing held-out test code.
rebar-implement Phase 4a/4b and rebar-brainstorm's Before-recording-tickets section reference the shared test-design.md for oracle design and AC shaping.
README.md documents the canonical-file + byte-identical-copies convention with the sync one-liner and drift-gate test, and no user- or machine-specific paths appear anywhere.
